title: eriogonum umbellatum subsp. subaridum
intro: Eriogonum umbellatum subsp. subaridum, also known as the sulphur buckwheat, is a plant species that belongs to the buckwheat family and is native to the southwestern region of North America. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Eriogonum umbellatum subsp. subaridum include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to treat wounds and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and can also be used in topical applications such as salves and creams to harness its medicinal properties.
